story.iWant: build-one, plan-one, plan-deck, and edit workflows to use the catalog
story.soThat: template selection is metadata-driven and consistent

tasks.task: Modify `build-one/instructions.md` template mapping section to read catalog.json
Implement catalog matching algorithm: ID -> use_cases -> fallback
Remove hardcoded template-to-file mappings in build-one
Modify `plan-one/instructions.md` to display templates from catalog.json
Modify `plan-deck/instructions.md` template suggestions to use catalog
Modify `edit/instructions.md` to reference catalog for template awareness
Implement fallback logging when no catalog match found
Test build-one with each starter template ID
Test build-one fallback to custom generation

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Given I run `/sb:build-one` with a template type (e.g., "layout-title"), when the workflow selects a template, then it: Reads `config/catalog/catalog.json`, Matches against template `id` first (exact match), Falls back to `use_cases` array match if no ID match, Loads the matched template HTML file from catalog/

Given I run `/sb:plan-one`, when I'm prompted to select a template, then: Available templates are read from catalog.json, Each template shows name and description, use_cases are used for intelligent suggestions

Given I run `/sb:plan-deck`, when templates are suggested for each slide, then: Suggestions reference catalog entry IDs, Template names and descriptions are used in output, Custom option always available for non-catalog needs
Given I run `/sb:edit`, when template context is needed, then: Reads catalog.json to understand available templates, Can reference template patterns for layout changes

Given a template type has no catalog match (ID or use_cases), when selection fails, then: Falls back to custom generation via frontend-design skill, Logs "No matching catalog template, using custom generation"

doc.title: Technical Specification: Template Catalog System
doc.snippet: Read catalog.json, parse templates array, match requested template type against id or use_cases, fallback to custom generation via frontend-design.

doc.title: Technical Specification: Template Catalog System
doc.snippet: Build-one reads catalog.json to find matching template. Plan-one shows available templates from catalog. Plan-deck references catalog for slide type suggestions.

doc.title: Technical Specification: Template Catalog System
doc.snippet: Each workflow.yaml needs: catalog_path: "{project-root}/.slide-builder/config/catalog" and catalog_manifest: "{catalog_path}/catalog.json"

doc.title: Epic 11: Template Catalog System
doc.snippet: Update build-one, plan-one, plan-deck, edit workflows to use catalog. Template selection is metadata-driven and consistent.

file.reason: Contains current hardcoded template-to-file mappings (layout-title: "01-title.html") that need to be replaced with catalog lookups
Template routing table references hardcoded sample files - needs to read from catalog.json instead
Reads sample HTML from config/samples/ - needs to read from config/catalog/ using catalog manifest
Contains hardcoded keyword-to-layout mappings - should reference catalog use_cases instead
Template matching rules use hardcoded keywords - should read from catalog.json use_cases
Static template reference table - should be dynamically generated from catalog.json
Hardcoded template mapping patterns - should reference catalog
Template matching uses hardcoded keyword patterns - should use catalog use_cases for intelligent matching
References config_path but not catalog - needs catalog_path and catalog_manifest variables
Uses frontend-design skill directly - should first check catalog for template patterns
The catalog manifest with template metadata (id, name, description, use_cases, file) that workflows should read

constraints.constraint: Template matching algorithm: ID exact match first, then use_cases array match, then fallback to custom via frontend-design skill
Catalog path variables must be added to all four workflow.yaml files
Fallback to custom generation must log "No matching catalog template, using custom generation"
Workflow YAML schema: name, description, installed_path, instructions required
Instruction files use XML tags within markdown: step, action, check, output
Slides must be 1920x1080 pixels with contenteditable and data-field attributes
Cannot break existing functionality - fallback to custom must always work
Prerequisites: Story 11.1 (catalog infrastructure), Story 11.2 (catalog populated with templates)

interface.name: Catalog Manifest Schema
interface.signature: { version: string, generated: string, lastModified: string, templates: Array<{id, name, description, use_cases: string[], file, preview, created_at, source}> }

interface.name: Catalog Matching Algorithm
interface.signature: matchTemplate(requestedType: string, catalog: CatalogManifest): { template: Template | null, matchType: 'id' | 'use_case' | 'fallback' }
interface.path: To be implemented in workflow instructions

interface.name: frontend-design skill
interface.signature: Skill tool with skill: "frontend-design", context includes current HTML, edit instruction, theme, constraints

tests.standards: No automated testing framework exists. Manual validation via running workflows and verifying output. Testing checklist includes: running each modified workflow end-to-end, verifying expected outputs created, checking status.yaml updates correctly, validating HTML output in browser at 1920x1080.

ideas.idea: Run `/sb:build-one` with template type "title" - verify it reads catalog.json and uses title.html template
Run `/sb:build-one` with template type "layout-title" - verify exact ID match works
Run `/sb:build-one` with intent "opening slide" - verify use_cases match finds title template
Run `/sb:build-one` with template "nonexistent-template" - verify fallback to custom generation with appropriate log message
Run `/sb:plan-one` - verify template options display shows catalog entries with names and descriptions
Run `/sb:plan-deck` - verify slide breakdown shows template suggestions from catalog
Run `/sb:edit` - verify template context available for layout changes
End-to-end test: setup -> plan-deck -> build-one -> edit cycle using catalog throughout
